headline: Australia’s Enervest shifts to own-and-operate model with 300MW battery storage system acquisition
summary_80w: Australian energy storage developer Enervest has acquired the Northern Border Battery, a 300MW battery energy storage system in development, from an unnamed Sydney-based developer.

headline: Malaysian utility TNB connects 100MW/400MWh grid-forming battery storage project to grid
summary_80w: Malaysian multinational utility company Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB) has launched a 100MW/400MWh battery storage project with an opening ceremony.

headline: Yarra Energy Foundation brings community battery paired with EV chargers online in Australia
summary_80w: Yarra Energy Foundation (YEF), a not-for-profit organisation, has commissioned a community battery storage system and two wheelchair-accessible electric vehicle (EV) charging bays in Clifton Hill, an inner-city suburb of Melbourne, Australia.

headline: Construction begins on OX2’s 300MWh Muswellbrook solar-plus-BESS in NSW, Australia
summary_80w: Swedish independent power producer (IPP) OX2 has confirmed it has started construction activities at the 100MW/300MWh Muswellbrook solar-plus-storage site in New South Wales (NSW), Australia.

headline: CIM Group launches US energy platform with 1.2GW solar PV, 690MW BESS
summary_80w: US real estate company CIM Group has launched an energy platform with a 2GW portfolio of solar PV and battery energy storage system (BESS) assets.

headline: Oman signs 2.7GW hybrid renewables PPA with Naqaa Sustainable Energy
summary_80w: Naqaa Sustainable Energy has signed a power purchase agreement (PPA) for a 2.7GW hybrid renewable energy project in Mahout and Duqm, Oman.

headline: Vietnam proposes 270MW new floating PV capacity
summary_80w: The Vietnamese state power utility Vietnam Energy Generation Corporation 1 (EVNGENCO1) has proposed the development of 270MW of floating solar PV capacity on three hydropower reservoirs.

headline: RUMSL launches 50MW and 200MW solar-storage tenders
summary_80w: RUMSL has launched two solar-plus-storage projects in India designed to provide power supply during peak demand periods.

headline: OX2 starts construction work at 135MW Muswellbrook solar-plus-storage project in Australia
summary_80w: OX2 has started construction work at its Muswellbrook project, which combines 135MW of solar capacity and 100MW of batteries.

headline: European Energy starts building 225MW agriPV project, Italy’s ‘largest’
summary_80w: Danish IPP European Energy has started constructing a 225.5MW agrivoltaic solar PV project in Sicily, which it claims will be the “largest” such project in Italy.

headline: ACEN Australia’s NSW solar portfolio delivers 87% generation boost in first quarter
summary_80w: ACEN Australia has revealed an 87% year-on-year increase in generation output for the first quarter of 2026, reaching 528GWh.

headline: Non-lithium players Eos and ESS lean on future of US LDES deployment in Q1 2026 financials
summary_80w: Two battery energy storage system (BESS) companies, Eos Energy and ESS Tech Inc, are betting big on the US adoption of long-duration energy storage (LDES) in Q1 2026 financial reports.

headline: Canadian Solar plans to double battery cell and BESS manufacturing capacity, new CEO says
summary_80w: Canadian Solar intends to double its battery energy storage system (BESS) division’s manufacturing capacity, the company’s new CEO has said.

headline: Australia hits 400,000 home battery installations with 11.2GWh capacity in under a year
summary_80w: Australia has surpassed 400,000 home battery storage installations under the federal government's Cheaper Home Batteries Program.

headline: India installs record 15.3GW of solar in Q1 2026 amid policy deadline rush
summary_80w: India installed a record 15.3GW of solar capacity in the first quarter of 2026, according to new data from market research firm Mercom.

headline: Australia: NSW firming tender secures 2,128MWh of energy storage to address summer shortfall
summary_80w: Australia’s New South Wales has seen 532MW of firming projects contracted through its seventh infrastructure tender, securing two projects designed to strengthen electricity supply during periods of peak demand and system stress in the Sydney-Newcastle-Wollongong region.

headline: ISC Konstanz advances plans for in-house solar cell and module pilot line
summary_80w: ISC Konstanz is upgrading its cleanroom facilities to operate a fully integrated solar cell and module pilot line by Q3 2026.

headline: Fujiyama Power commissions 2GW solar module manufacturing plant
summary_80w: Indian rooftop solar company Fujiyama Power has commissioned a 2GW solar module manufacturing facility in Ratlam, Madhya Pradesh.

headline: Are US manufacturers accelerating their protectionist stance?
summary_80w: PV Tech Premium analyses whether this new PV trade scrutiny on Ethiopia could be a sign of accelerated protectionism from US manufacturers.

headline: The UK has space for corporate PPAs in its portfolio of operational solar assets
summary_80w: While CfDs are the most attractive route to market in UK solar, EDF's Ross Irvine says that there are opportunities for corporate PPAs.

headline: Meridian Energy bags consent for 120MW New Zealand solar plant
summary_80w: New Zealand utility Meridian Energy has received consent to build a 120MW solar PV project alongside a planned battery energy storage system (BESS).

headline: Construction begins on AU$72 million solar-powered green hydrogen hub in New South Wales, Australia
summary_80w: Construction has commenced on New South Wales’ (NSW) first integrated green hydrogen and ammonia production facility in Australia.

headline: Solomon Islands partners with ADB to develop first large-scale solar PV plant
summary_80w: ADB and Solomon Islands Electricity Authority (SIEA) have signed an agreement to develop the country's first large-scale solar PV plant.

headline: Zelestra energises 1GWh hybrid BESS project in Chile
summary_80w: Spanish developer and independent power producer (IPP) Zelestra has energised its 1GWh battery energy storage system (BESS) in the Atacama Desert in Chile.

headline: Rept Battero opens Indonesian cell and BESS manufacturing facility
summary_80w: China-based Rept Battero has officially opened its lithium-ion cell and BESS manufacturing facility in Indonesia.

headline: System-level BESS manufacturing defects cause ‘hidden risk’ for developers, Intertek CEA says
summary_80w: The “vast majority” of issues identified in Intertek CEA’s 2025 audits were system-level defects that could pose risks to projects if undetected and unresolved.

headline: EnerVenue piloting ‘30,000-cycle’ nickel-hydrogen BESS in China
summary_80w: Nickel-hydrogen battery company EnerVenue has announced a pilot battery energy storage system (BESS) project in Jintan, Changzhou, China.

headline: Iberdrola Australia submits 1GW battery storage system to the EPBC Act

headline: Arava Power acquires half of OCI Energy’s La Salle solar project, currently under construction in Texas

headline: Pew’s US DER policy playbook aimed at giving ‘every state a path forward’
summary_80w: Energy-Storage.news Premium speaks with Pat Wood III, co-chair at Pew Charitable Trusts about the company’s DER Policy Playbook

headline: Akaysha Energy bags EPBC Act approval for 1,600MWh battery storage system in Victoria, Australia
summary_80w: Developer Akaysha Energy has received federal environmental clearance for its 400MW/1,600MWh Glenrowan battery energy storage system (BESS) in Victoria, with the project deemed "not a controlled action" under Australia's Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation (EPBC) Act.

headline: MN8 Energy bags US$300 million to extend credit facility
summary_80w: MN8 Energy has raised US$300 million to extend a corporate credit facility that will build out its pipeline of US solar and storage projects.

headline: Canadian Solar module shipments fall to 2.5GW in Q1 2026 as Colin Parkin transitions to CEO
summary_80w: Canadian Solar has posted a quarter-on-quarter decline in both solar module shipments and net revenues in the first quarter of 2026.

headline: Breaking it down: Kiwa PVEL expands test to failure methods for assessing module breakage risk
summary_80w: Kiwa PVEL has rolled out updates to its module testing programme, with two changes affecting Static Mechanical Load (SML) and hail testing.

headline: DNV launches technical standards for floating solar
summary_80w: Norway-headquartered technical and safety expert DNV has published two new standards for the design and maintenance of floating solar PV (FPV) systems.

headline: New Zealand launches sector review to streamline residential solar approvals
summary_80w: New Zealand's government has ordered a sector review into the installation of residential and small to medium-scale solar, aiming to reduce what it describes as a "red tape nightmare" that can delay approvals for months.

headline: China’s Gotion and Richardson Electronics partner for US-made BESS
summary_80w: China-headquartered energy storage firm Gotion and US power electronics manufacturer Richardson Electronics have partnered to manufacture BESS. It comes at a time when Chinese companies are starting to sell down stakes in US assets amid new FEOC rules.

headline: Hydrostor to enter A-CAES project into Ontario LDES procurement
summary_80w: Long-duration energy storage (LDES) developer-operator Hydrostor intends to enter its compressed air storage project into Ontario, Canada’s recently announced LDES procurement.

headline: Nearly 13GWh of grid-scale BESS deployed globally in April
summary_80w: A total of 4.5GW/12.8GWh of large-scale BESS capacity when into operation around the world last month, with Asia and South America showing strong figures.

headline: INDIA Roundup: ACME Solar, IndiGrid commission Rajasthan & Gujarat BESS projects, GE Vernova wins pumped hydro contract
summary_80w: Battery storage and pumped hydro projects in India, in this edition of news in brief from around the world in energy storage.

headline: Battery Breakthrough Initiative hit as Australia cuts AU$1.3 billion in clean energy funding
summary_80w: Australia has clawed back AU$1.3 billion (US$940 million) in uncommitted clean energy manufacturing funding, with the Solar Sunshot Program and Battery Breakthrough Initiative among the programmes hit by budget cuts announced this week.

headline: T1 Energy registers record quarterly net income, produces 683MW solar modules in Q1
summary_80w: US solar manufacturer T1 Energy has registered a record quarterly net income and adjusted EBITDA in the first quarter of 2026.

headline: Meta inks 850MW US solar and storage PPAs with DESRI

headline: RWE expands Illinois footprint with 273MW solar project

headline: Hailstorms, stow methods and fire lead US solar project risks in 2026 Solar Risk Assessment
summary_80w: Solar PV project performance in the US can be significantly impacted by the impacts of hailstorms, stowing methods and fire.

headline: SMA Solar reports Q1 loss, fine-tunes 2026 guidance

headline: Nextpower to acquire Zigor and Apex Power inverter assets in US$80.5 million deal

headline: Galileo CEO on ‘completely different’ development market, meeting PPA demand and where in Europe it will transact next

headline: US ROUNDUP: BESS project updates from Spearmint, Polaris, and Clearway in Texas, Puerto Rico and Utah

headline: IRENA: Renewables with storage cost-competitive with fossil fuels for round-the-clock power
summary_80w: Solar PV and wind are now the cheapest sources of power, with co-located hybrids increasingly delivering round-the-clock electricity at fossil fuel-competitive costs in high-resource regions, according to a new report by IRENA.

headline: Western Australia’s battery storage supplies record 37.2% of peak demand

headline: Grenergy bags further funding for 342MW/1,034MWh Chile solar-plus-storage

headline: GameChange, Raptor Maps partner on automated solar plant monitoring system

headline: Poor commercial and technology decisions could undermine the promise of co-located projects

headline: SEIA appoints former Republican governor as new CEO

headline: Ford officially launches US stationary energy storage subsidiary, deliveries to begin in 2027

headline: BayWa r.e. to manage Germany’s largest BESS; smaller projects move to construction

summary_80w: Developer Alternate Path has secured federal environmental approval for its 500MW/2,000MWh Murchs Corner battery energy storage system (BESS) in Victoria, Australia.

summary_80w: Amid the PV industry's toughest downturn, JA Solar held its 2025 annual results briefing on May 6 2026, offering the market a key glimpse of when the sector may turn the corner.

headline: PV module recycling critical as ‘we will use all the silver in the world in five years’, warns UNSW professor

headline: Aboriginal-led energy group closes financing on 150MW Jinbi solar plant in Western Australia

headline: Premium PV module prices rise again in Europe despite softer April demand signals
summary_80w: Despite softening demand momentum, premium solar module prices across Europe continued to rise in April.

headline: Binhai Energy terminates 15GW ingot/cell manufacturing plant, redirects resources to battery sector
summary_80w: The company has formally terminated its originally planned 15GW ingot pulling and PV cell manufacturing project, redirecting its resources to the more promising lithium battery silicon-carbon anode material sector.

headline: BloombergNEF forecasts 158GW of global energy storage deployments in 2026

summary_80w: Leading European BESS owner-operators discuss the roles of subsidy schemes, contract revenues and merchant approaches in helping the continent build out its BESS pipeline.

headline: AGL’s Liddell and Tomago battery storage systems to boost returns by 10% in Australia
summary_80w: Australian utility AGL has said adding its Liddell and Tomago BESS to its existing coal-fired generation could boost portfolio returns by 10%

headline: US BESS operators face growing complexity as markets mature

headline: Organic flow battery company CMBlu closes €50 million Series C

summary_80w: Large-scale BESS projects have been completed or progressed in Bulgaria, Moldova, North Macedonia and Romania, including what is now the largest operational system in the CEE region.

summary_80w: Sungrow’s BESS and grid-forming power conversion system (PCS) combined established system voltage within 19 seconds during black-start testing at the company’s newly established large-scale, plant-level grid-forming field test base.

headline: Australia installs 10.7GWh of home battery storage under federal subsidy scheme
summary_80w: Australia has installed 380,712 home battery storage systems, representing 10.7GWh of storage capacity, since the introduction of the Cheaper Home Batteries Program last year, Chris Bowen, Australia's minister for climate change and energy, has disclosed.
